frameworks.name: linear threshold heuristic
frameworks.one_line: Position-dependent linear threshold governs accept/reject in sequential search.

frameworks.name: optimal stopping theory
frameworks.one_line: Normative position-dependent threshold strategy that humans diverge from.

core_question: Why do human stopping decisions in sequential search diverge from the optimal strategy, and can a linear threshold heuristic explain them?
key_assumptions: options are encountered sequentially without return to rejected ones
stopping decisions depend on current position in the sequence
heuristic threshold is a linear function of position
manipulations: task environment / option distribution across studies
real-world vs laboratory sequential decision setting
key_findings: linear threshold model fits human stopping behavior better than existing models
model accurately predicts search behavior across different environments
model generalizes to a real-world sequential decision problem
interpretations: humans implement a linear threshold heuristic rather than the optimal nonlinear rule
this heuristic captures the systematic divergence from optimality in sequential search
